• Main Page
  • Modules
  • Data Structures
  • Files
  • Examples

gxpci PCIe and StreamIO Data Transfer API

Detailed Description

The gxpci library allows applications to post zero copy data transfer commands to the Gx PCIe ports.

The gxpci library provides application control of data transfer operations among the PCIe ports using the PCIe and StreamIO protocols.

The Gx Chip-to-Chip (C2C), host NIC and host Packet Queue API allows applications to post PCIe zero-copy commands to an unidirectional data transfer queue which is defined by the queue type gxpci_queue_type_t, the queue index and link index of the two peer PCIe ports. The API eliminates the Linux system calls from the data transfer process and is able to achieve low overhead and high throughput.

The Gx C2C and H2T/T2H API libgxpci is implemented on top of the libgxio API for allocating, configuring, and manipulating Gx TRIO hardware resources.

Initialization

First, bind each process that will communicate from a Gx processor to a unique core, so that each process cannot migrate to another core during the course of its execution.

Second, each process invokes the libgxio gxio_trio_init() to allocate a TRIO context or "service domain". The initialized TRIO context will be passed to all libgxpci API functions. gxio_trio_init() is the only libgxio function that needs to called explicitly by the application.

Next, invoke gxpci_init() to allocate a gxpci context which includes a reference to the aforementioned TRIO context. The gxpci context is also associated with the local PCIe port whose MAC index is passed to gxpci_init(). Note that one gxpci context is associated with one process or thread that should monopolize a single core. It is suggested that multiple worker threads could feed into a service thread that owns the gxpci context.

Next, invoke gxpci_open_queue() to create a data transfer queue, passing in queue type, queue index within that type and the remote PCIe port link index. The GXPCI_NIC_H2T and GXPCI_NIC_T2H types specify the H2T/T2H queues while GXPCI_C2C_SEND and GXPCI_C2C_RECV types create C2C queues.

Management

The gxpci API requires that applications use special "registered" memory to store their packet data. Memory is registered via the gxpci_iomem_register() function, which takes the virtual address of a range of huge page memory and sets up the VA/PA mapping needed by the TRIO engine.

Transfer

At this point, the process can use gxpci_post_cmd() to post commands and gxpci_get_comps() to get completions for those commands. Since there are a limited number of commands outstanding on any communication queue, trying to have more commands outstanding than the limit will cause the gxpci_post_cmd() to return error type GXPCI_ECREDITS which is a hint to the application to retry the command.

Reset and Resource Deallocation

Once a queue is reset, all SW and HW resources associated with this queue are released. The queue must start from scratch to run again. If the reset is initiated by the tile application, it should simply call gxpci_destroy() function. If the reset is initiated by the remote end, the reset condition will be detected by checking for return value GXPCI_ERESET from functions gxpci_open_queue(), gxpci_post_cmd() and gxpci_get_comps(). Upon detecting the queue reset event, the tile application should call gxpci_destroy() before exiting or restarting.

Examples

See $TILERA_ROOT/examples/trio/gxpci_c2c/c2c_send.c for an example of chip-to-chip zero-copy messaging using the gxpci API.

The gxpci API is designed to achieve high throughput and low latency by moving data directly between the user sapce buffers and the PCIe ports without extra memory copies.

Applications can link with the gxpci library by specifying the -lgxpci compiler flag. Because the gxpci library has a dependency on the gxio library, -lgxio compiler flag also needs to be specified.

Data Structures

struct  gxpci_cmd_t
 A structure used to post zero-copy commands. More...
struct  gxpci_dma_cmd_t
 A structure used to post DMA commands. More...
struct  gxpci_comp_t
 The structure returned when a command completes. More...
struct  gxpci_nic_comp_t
 The structure returned when a host NIC command completes. More...
struct  gxpci_nic_state_s
 A gxpci_nic_state_t describes the NIC-wide state, as opposed to queue-specific resource structure gxpci_resource_t. More...
struct  c2c_pkt_header
 We need to embed a chip-to-chip packet header in the beginning of each packet for the following reasons: -- to transport the size of packet data payload to the receiver, in the absence of dedicated metadata packets. More...
struct  gxpci_raw_dma_state_s
 This describes the Raw DMA channel-wide state, as opposed to queue-specific resource structure gxpci_resource_t. More...
struct  gxpci_packet_queue_state
 This describes the Packet Queue unidirectional queue state. More...
struct  gxpci_resource_s
 A gxpci_resource_t describes the resources that are allocated to a ZC client. More...
struct  gxpci_context_s
 A gxpci_context_t describes a unidirectional ZC queue. More...

Defines

#define VFS_SHARE_AN_ASID   4
 Max number of VFs that share a single ASID.
#define MAX_DMA_DESC_RING_BUF   (1 << 20)
 Max size for each Push or Pull DMA descriptor ring buffer.
#define GXPCI_RAW_DMA_BACK_MEM_SIZE   tmc_alloc_get_huge_pagesize()
 Below is the Raw DMA queue definitions.
#define GXPCI_HOST_RD_REGS_OFFSET   0x0
 The Raw DMA queue's host MMIO registers' offset in the backing memory.
#define GXPCI_HOST_RD_DMA_CMDS_BUF_OFFSET   0x200000
 The Raw DMA queue's data DMA command ring buffer's offset in the backing memory.
#define GXPCI_QUEUE_INDEX_INTERFACE_MASK   0xffff
 In some applications, such as the host NIC interface, the queue_index field of gxpci_context_t consists of two indices: the interface index at the low half-word and the queue index at the high half-word.
#define GXPCI_QUEUE_INDEX_QUEUE_SHIFT   16
 The shift of the queue index part.

Typedefs

typedef enum gxpci_queue_type_e gxpci_queue_type_t
 Different types of zero-copy queues.
typedef struct gxpci_nic_state_s gxpci_nic_state_t
 A gxpci_nic_state_t describes the NIC-wide state, as opposed to queue-specific resource structure gxpci_resource_t.
typedef struct c2c_pkt_header c2c_pkt_header_t
 We need to embed a chip-to-chip packet header in the beginning of each packet for the following reasons: -- to transport the size of packet data payload to the receiver, in the absence of dedicated metadata packets.
typedef struct
gxpci_raw_dma_state_s 		gxpci_raw_dma_state_t
 This describes the Raw DMA channel-wide state, as opposed to queue-specific resource structure gxpci_resource_t.
typedef struct gxpci_resource_s gxpci_resource_t
 A gxpci_resource_t describes the resources that are allocated to a ZC client.
typedef struct gxpci_context_s gxpci_context_t
 A gxpci_context_t describes a unidirectional ZC queue.

Enumerations

enum  gxpci_queue_type_e {
  GXPCI_NIC_H2T, GXPCI_NIC_T2H, GXPCI_PQ_H2T, GXPCI_PQ_T2H,
  GXPCI_PQ_DUPLEX, GXPCI_C2C_SEND, GXPCI_C2C_RECV, GXPCI_C2C_DUPLEX,
  GXPCI_RAW_DMA_SEND, GXPCI_RAW_DMA_RECV, GXPCI_PQ_H2T_VF, GXPCI_PQ_T2H_VF,
  GXPCI_PQ_DUPLEX_VF
}
 Different types of zero-copy queues. More...
enum  gxpci_err_e {
  GXPCI_ERR_MAX = -1301, GXPCI_EINVAL = -1301, GXPCI_EBINDCPU = -1302, GXPCI_ECREDITS = -1303,
  GXPCI_ERESET = -1304, GXPCI_ERR_MIN = -1304
}
 Error codes that may be returned by the PCI zero-copy command posting APIs. More...

Functions

int gxpci_init (gxio_trio_context_t *trio_context, gxpci_context_t *context, unsigned int trio_index, unsigned int mac)
 Create a communication endpoint which is used to post data transfer commands, wait for transfer completions.
int gxpci_open_queue (gxpci_context_t *context, int asid, gxpci_queue_type_t type, unsigned int rem_link_index, unsigned int queue_index, unsigned int pkt_headroom, unsigned int recv_buf_size)
 Open a selected queue between the local PCIe port and a remote PCIe port.
int gxpci_open_duplex_queue (gxpci_context_t *h2t_context, gxpci_context_t *t2h_context, int asid, gxpci_queue_type_t type, unsigned int rem_link_index, unsigned int queue_index)
 Open a selected duplex queue between the local PCIe port and a remote PCIe port.
int gxpci_post_cmd (gxpci_context_t *context, const gxpci_cmd_t *cmd)
 Post a command.
int gxpci_pq_t2h_cmd (gxpci_context_t *context, const gxpci_cmd_t *cmd)
 Post a command to the tile-to-host packet queue.
int gxpci_pq_h2t_cmd (gxpci_context_t *context, const gxpci_cmd_t *cmd)
 Post a command to the host-to-tile packet queue.
int gxpci_raw_dma_send_cmd (gxpci_context_t *context, const gxpci_dma_cmd_t *cmd)
 Post a command to the Raw DMA transmit queue.
int gxpci_raw_dma_recv_cmd (gxpci_context_t *context, const gxpci_dma_cmd_t *cmd)
 Post a command to the Raw DMA receive queue.
int gxpci_get_comps (gxpci_context_t *context, gxpci_comp_t *comps, int min, int max)
 Retrieve command completions.
int gxpci_get_cmd_credits (gxpci_context_t *context)
 Retrieve the number of command credits, i.e.
int gxpci_iomem_register (gxpci_context_t *context, void *va, size_t size)
 Register huge-page-size memory with the PCIe system for data transfer.
int gxpci_destroy (gxpci_context_t *context)
 Release the communication endpoint and free the allocated resources.
int gxpci_destroy_duplex (gxpci_context_t *h2t_context, gxpci_context_t *t2h_context, gxpci_queue_type_t type)
 Release the duplex communication endpoint and free the allocated resources.
const char * gxpci_strerror (int gxpci_errno)
 Translate a gxpci error code into a string.
int gxpci_c2c_open_send_queue (gxpci_context_t *context, unsigned int send_queue_index)

int gxpci_c2c_open_recv_queue (gxpci_context_t *context, unsigned int pkt_headroom, unsigned int recv_page_size, void *recv_page_addr, unsigned int recv_queue_index)

int gxpci_pq_destroy (gxpci_context_t *context)

Define Documentation

#define VFS_SHARE_AN_ASID   4

Max number of VFs that share a single ASID.

#define MAX_DMA_DESC_RING_BUF   (1 << 20)

Max size for each Push or Pull DMA descriptor ring buffer.

This is set to 1MB because hardware supports max RING_ORDs of 16 (65536 entries), with each entry being 16-byte gxio_trio_dma_desc_t. This is used by the VFs in sharing a huge page to store their DMA descriptor rings.

#define GXPCI_RAW_DMA_BACK_MEM_SIZE   tmc_alloc_get_huge_pagesize()

Below is the Raw DMA queue definitions.

The Raw DMA queue backing memory size.

#define GXPCI_HOST_RD_REGS_OFFSET   0x0

The Raw DMA queue's host MMIO registers' offset in the backing memory.

#define GXPCI_HOST_RD_DMA_CMDS_BUF_OFFSET   0x200000

The Raw DMA queue's data DMA command ring buffer's offset in the backing memory.

#define GXPCI_QUEUE_INDEX_INTERFACE_MASK   0xffff

In some applications, such as the host NIC interface, the queue_index field of gxpci_context_t consists of two indices: the interface index at the low half-word and the queue index at the high half-word.

One interface has one or more channels. Each channel is either a xmit/recv queue pair or a unidirectional queue.

#define GXPCI_QUEUE_INDEX_QUEUE_SHIFT   16

The shift of the queue index part.

Typedef Documentation

typedef enum gxpci_queue_type_e gxpci_queue_type_t

Different types of zero-copy queues.

Each queue provides unidirectional data transfer path between two Tile Processors or a Tile Processor and the host machine. The unidirectional nature allows for fine-grained hardware resource allocation.

typedef struct gxpci_nic_state_s gxpci_nic_state_t

A gxpci_nic_state_t describes the NIC-wide state, as opposed to queue-specific resource structure gxpci_resource_t.

This should contain state information that is used once at app init time.

typedef struct c2c_pkt_header c2c_pkt_header_t

We need to embed a chip-to-chip packet header in the beginning of each packet for the following reasons: -- to transport the size of packet data payload to the receiver, in the absence of dedicated metadata packets.

The sender application should set the data payload size in this header. The PCIe packet payload size is the data payload size plus the c2c_pkt_header_t size. -- to include a packet availability flag informing the receiver of the the packet arrival. This flag should also be set by the sender application.

typedef struct gxpci_raw_dma_state_s gxpci_raw_dma_state_t

This describes the Raw DMA channel-wide state, as opposed to queue-specific resource structure gxpci_resource_t.

This should contain state information that is used once at app init time. A Raw DMA channel may include multiple T2H and H2T queues.

typedef struct gxpci_resource_s gxpci_resource_t

A gxpci_resource_t describes the resources that are allocated to a ZC client.

Each client has one gxpci_resource_t. Note that the types of resources allocated for a client depends on the queue type.

A GXPCI_NIC_H2T queue requires the following resources: push-DMA, pull-DMA, Memory Map A GXPCI_NIC_T2H queue requires the following resources: push-DMA, Memory Map, pull-DMA A GXPCI_PQ_H2T queue requires the following resources: pull-DMA, Memory Map, PIO A GXPCI_PQ_T2H queue requires the following resources: push-DMA, Memory Map, PIO A GXPCI_C2C_SEND queue requires the following resources: push-DMA, Memory Map A GXPCI_C2C_RECV queue requires the following resources: Scatter_Queue, Memory Map, PIO

typedef struct gxpci_context_s gxpci_context_t

A gxpci_context_t describes a unidirectional ZC queue.

Each ZC client instance has one gxpci_context_t.

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum gxpci_queue_type_e

Different types of zero-copy queues.

Each queue provides unidirectional data transfer path between two Tile Processors or a Tile Processor and the host machine. The unidirectional nature allows for fine-grained hardware resource allocation.

Enumerator:
GXPCI_NIC_H2T  Host to TILE: this is the egress queue of the host NIC interface.

GXPCI_NIC_T2H  TILE to host: this is the ingress queue of the host NIC interface.

GXPCI_PQ_H2T  Packet Queue H2T: this is the endpoint reading data from the host.

GXPCI_PQ_T2H  Packet Queue T2H: this is the endpoint writing data to the host.

GXPCI_PQ_DUPLEX  Packet Queue Duplex: data transfer between the host and the endpoint.

GXPCI_C2C_SEND  TILE to TILE: this is the sender TILE PCIe port.

GXPCI_C2C_RECV  TILE to TILE: this is the receiver TILE PCIe port.

GXPCI_C2C_DUPLEX  TILE to TILE: bi-directional transfer queues.

GXPCI_RAW_DMA_SEND  Raw DMA: this is the sender.

GXPCI_RAW_DMA_RECV  Raw DMA: this is the receiver.

GXPCI_PQ_H2T_VF  Packet Queue H2T for SR-IOV VF.

GXPCI_PQ_T2H_VF  Packet Queue T2H for SR-IOV VF.

GXPCI_PQ_DUPLEX_VF  Duplex PQ for VF: data transfer between a guest and the endpoint.

enum gxpci_err_e

Error codes that may be returned by the PCI zero-copy command posting APIs.

Such functions return 0 on success and a negative value if an error occurs.

In cases where a gxpci function failed due to a error reported by system libraries, the error code will be the negation of the system errno at the time of failure. The gxpci_strerror() function will deliver error strings for both gxpci and system error codes.

Enumerator:
GXPCI_ERR_MAX  Largest gxpci error number.

GXPCI_EINVAL  Invalid parameter.

GXPCI_EBINDCPU  Process must be bound to a single cpu to invoke gxpci APIs.

GXPCI_ECREDITS  Insufficient command credits to post command.

GXPCI_ERESET  The queue is being reset.

GXPCI_ERR_MIN  Smallest gxpci error number.

Function Documentation

int gxpci_init ( gxio_trio_context_t *  trio_context,
gxpci_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  trio_index,
unsigned int  mac 
)

Create a communication endpoint which is used to post data transfer commands, wait for transfer completions.

Parameters:
[in] trio_context Pointer to a pre-allocated gxio_trio_context_t struct.
[out] context Pointer to a context object to be initialized. Once initialized, this object is supplied to other gxpci routines.
[in] trio_index The TRIO index of the local PCIe port, Gx36 must pass 0.
[in] mac The mac number of the local PCIe port, e.g. 0, 1 or 2.
Returns:
0 on success, GXPCI_EINVAL if either trio_index or mac is invalid.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_c2c/c2c_send.c, trio/gxpci_host_pq/host.c, and trio/gxpci_mmi/mmi.c.

int gxpci_open_queue ( gxpci_context_t *  context,
int  asid,
gxpci_queue_type_t  type,
unsigned int  rem_link_index,
unsigned int  queue_index,
unsigned int  pkt_headroom,
unsigned int  recv_buf_size 
)

Open a selected queue between the local PCIe port and a remote PCIe port.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized context object.
[in] asid The ASID if pre-allocated, else GXIO_ASID_NULL.
[in] type The zero-copy queue type.
[in] rem_link_index The global PCIe link index of the target PCIe port of the chip-to-chip queue, when the queue is opened by a Gx PCIe physical function. This argument specifies the SR-IOV virtual function number if the queue is opened by a Gx PCIe VF.
[in] queue_index The index of the queue.
[in] pkt_headroom The receive packet headroom size in chip-to-chip queues. It must not be larger than the value in the recv_buf_size argument. For queue types other than GXPCI_C2C_RECV or GXPCI_NIC_H2T, it is ignored.
[in] recv_buf_size The receive buffer size in chip-to-chip queues. It must not be larger than GXPCI_C2C_MAX_RECV_BUF_SIZE. For queue types other than GXPCI_C2C_RECV, this is ignored.
Returns:
0 on success, -EBUSY if the queue resource is not available, GXPCI_ERESET if the queue is reset by the remote end, GXPCI_EBINDCPU if process is not bound to a single cpu, or an error value of type gxio_err_e.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_c2c/c2c_send.c, and trio/gxpci_host_pq/host.c.

int gxpci_open_duplex_queue ( gxpci_context_t *  h2t_context,
gxpci_context_t *  t2h_context,
int  asid,
gxpci_queue_type_t  type,
unsigned int  rem_link_index,
unsigned int  queue_index 
)

Open a selected duplex queue between the local PCIe port and a remote PCIe port.

Parameters:
[in] h2t_context Initialized context object of H2T direction for queue types GXPCI_PQ_DUPLEX and GXPCI_PQ_DUPLEX_VF. For queue type GXPCI_C2C_DUPLEX, this is the context object for the receive queue.
[in] t2h_context Initialized context object of T2H direction for queue type GXPCI_PQ_DUPLEX and GXPCI_PQ_DUPLEX_VF. For queue type GXPCI_C2C_DUPLEX, this is the context object for the send queue.
[in] asid The ASID if pre-allocated, else GXIO_ASID_NULL.
[in] type The duplex queue type.
[in] rem_link_index The global PCIe link index of the target PCIe port for queue type GXPCI_C2C_DUPLEX. For queue type GXPCI_PQ_DUPLEX_VF, this is the SR-IOV virtual function instance number.
[in] queue_index The index of the duplex queue for queue types GXPCI_PQ_DUPLEX and GXPCI_PQ_DUPLEX_VF, ignored for other queue types.
Returns:
0 on success, -EBUSY if the queue resource is not available, GXPCI_ERESET if the queue is reset by the remote end, or an error value of type gxio_err_e.

int gxpci_post_cmd ( gxpci_context_t *  context,
const gxpci_cmd_t *  cmd 
)

Post a command.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized context object.
[in] cmd Pointer to a command.
Returns:
0 if the command is posted, or GXPCI_ECREDITS if this queue does not have enough command credits to post the command or in the case of send queues if the send completion array is full, or GXPCI_ERESET if the queue is reset by the remote end.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_c2c/c2c_send.c.

int gxpci_pq_t2h_cmd ( gxpci_context_t *  context,
const gxpci_cmd_t *  cmd 
)

Post a command to the tile-to-host packet queue.

The performance requirement for packet queues dictates that this function should be accessable from the application directly.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized context object.
[in] cmd Pointer to a command.
Returns:
0 if the command is posted, or GXPCI_ECREDITS if this queue does not have enough command credits to post the command, or GXPCI_ERESET if the queue is reset by the remote end.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_host_pq/host.c.

int gxpci_pq_h2t_cmd ( gxpci_context_t *  context,
const gxpci_cmd_t *  cmd 
)

Post a command to the host-to-tile packet queue.

The performance requirement for packet queues dictates that this function should be accessable from the application directly.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized context object.
[in] cmd Pointer to a command.
Returns:
0 if the command is posted, or GXPCI_ECREDITS if this queue does not have enough command credits to post the command, or GXPCI_ERESET if the queue is reset by the remote end.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_host_pq/host.c.

int gxpci_raw_dma_send_cmd ( gxpci_context_t *  context,
const gxpci_dma_cmd_t *  cmd 
)

Post a command to the Raw DMA transmit queue.

Post a command to a Raw DMA send queue.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized context object.
[in] cmd Pointer to a command.
Returns:
0 if the command is posted, or GXPCI_ERESET if the queue is reset by the remote end.
Parameters:
[in] context Initialized gxpci_context_t struct.
[in] cmd Pointer to a command.
Returns:
0 on success, or GXPCI_ERESET if the queue is reset.

int gxpci_raw_dma_recv_cmd ( gxpci_context_t *  context,
const gxpci_dma_cmd_t *  cmd 
)

Post a command to the Raw DMA receive queue.

Post a command to a Raw DMA receive queue.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized context object.
[in] cmd Pointer to a command.
Returns:
0 if the command is posted, or GXPCI_ERESET if the queue is reset by the remote end.
Parameters:
[in] context Initialized gxpci_context_t struct.
[in] cmd Pointer to a command.
Returns:
0 on success, or GXPCI_ERESET if the queue is reset.

int gxpci_get_comps ( gxpci_context_t *  context,
gxpci_comp_t *  comps,
int  min,
int  max 
)

Retrieve command completions.

This function will block if there are fewer than the minimal number of completions which is specified by "min". It returns immediately if min is zero even if there are no completions.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized context object.
[in] comps Pointer to completion packet array.
[in] min Wait for at least this many completions.
[in] max The maximum number of completions to return.
Returns:
The number of commands completed and copied into the comps array or GXPCI_ERESET if the queue is reset by the remote end.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_c2c/c2c_send.c, and trio/gxpci_host_pq/host.c.

int gxpci_get_cmd_credits ( gxpci_context_t *  context  ) 

Retrieve the number of command credits, i.e.

free command descriptors.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized context object.
Returns:
The number of command credits in command queue on success, or GXPCI_EINVAL if queue type is invalid.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_host_pq/host.c.

int gxpci_iomem_register ( gxpci_context_t *  context,
void *  va,
size_t  size 
)

Register huge-page-size memory with the PCIe system for data transfer.

This memory is used for packet buffers.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized context object.
[in] va Data buffer address.
[in] size Data buffer size.
Returns:
0 on success, an error value returned by gxio_trio_register_page().
Examples:
trio/gxpci_c2c/c2c_send.c, and trio/gxpci_host_pq/host.c.

int gxpci_destroy ( gxpci_context_t *  context  ) 

Release the communication endpoint and free the allocated resources.

These resources include both SW resources and TRIO HW resources. These resources will be released implicitly when the application exits without calling gxpci_destroy() or is terminated.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized context object.
Returns:
0 on success, non-zero on error.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_host_pq/host.c.

int gxpci_destroy_duplex ( gxpci_context_t *  h2t_context,
gxpci_context_t *  t2h_context,
gxpci_queue_type_t  type 
)

Release the duplex communication endpoint and free the allocated resources.

These resources include both SW resources and TRIO HW resources. These resources will be released implicitly when the application exits without calling gxpci_destroy_duplex() or is terminated.

Parameters:
[in] h2t_context Initialized context object of H2T direction.
[in] t2h_context Initialized context object of T2H direction.
[in] type The zero-copy duplex queue type.
Returns:
0 on success, non-zero on error.

const char* gxpci_strerror ( int  gxpci_errno  ) 

Translate a gxpci error code into a string.

Parameters:
[in] gxpci_errno An error number returned from a gxpci API call.
Returns:
A string describing the requested gxpci error number. If the error number corresponds to a gxio error, system error or is invalid, the gxio_strerror() result for the error number is returned.

int gxpci_c2c_open_send_queue ( gxpci_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  send_queue_index 
)

The following prototypes are for the new Chip-to-Chip API implementation that supports 64 C2C queues.

Open a chip-to-chip sender queue.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized gxpci_context_t struct for the sender queue.
[in] send_queue_index The index of the sender queue.
Returns:
0 on success, non-zero on error.

int gxpci_c2c_open_recv_queue ( gxpci_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  pkt_headroom,
unsigned int  recv_page_size,
void *  recv_page_addr,
unsigned int  recv_queue_index 
)

Open a chip-to-chip receiver queue.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized gxpci_context_t struct for the receiver queue.
[in] pkt_headroom The receive packet headroom size in a chip-to-chip receiver queue, indicating the offset from the beginning of the receive buffer where data will be written to.
[in] recv_page_size The size of the single page that is allocated by the receiver application to serve as the receive buffer pool. Usually, this is size of the huge page, i.e. 16MB.
[in] recv_page_addr The address of the single page that is allocated by the receiver application to serve as the receive buffer pool.
[in] recv_queue_index The index of the receiver queue.
Returns:
0 on success, non-zero on error.

int gxpci_c2c_send_cmds ( gxpci_context_t *  context,
const gxpci_cmd_t *  cmds,
uint32_t  cmd_count 
)

Post some commands from a command array to a chip-to-chip sender queue.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized gxpci_context_t struct for the sender queue.
[in] cmds Pointer to a command array.
[in] cmd_count Number of commands to be posted.
Returns:
0 on success, or GXPCI_ERESET if the queue is reset.

int gxpci_c2c_recv_cmds ( gxpci_context_t *  context,
const gxpci_cmd_t *  cmds,
uint32_t  cmd_count 
)

Post some commands from a command array to a chip-to-chip receiver queue.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized gxpci_context_t struct for the receiver queue.
[in] cmds Pointer to a command array.
[in] cmd_count Number of commands to be posted.
Returns:
0 on success, or GXPCI_ERESET if the queue is reset.

int gxpci_c2c_get_send_comps ( gxpci_context_t *  context,
gxpci_comp_t *  cpls,
int  min,
int  max 
)

Retrieve command completions from a chip-to-chip sender queue.

This function will block if there are fewer than the minimal number of completions which is specified by "min". It returns immediately if min is zero even if there are no completions.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized context object.
[in] cpls Pointer to completion packet array.
[in] min Wait for at least this many completions.
[in] max The maximum number of completions to return.
Returns:
The number of commands completed and copied into the comps array or GXPCI_ERESET if the queue is reset by the remote end.

int gxpci_c2c_get_recv_comps ( gxpci_context_t *  context,
gxpci_comp_t *  cpls,
int  min,
int  max 
)

Retrieve command completions from a chip-to-chip receiver queue.

This function will block if there are fewer than the minimal number of completions which is specified by "min". It returns immediately if min is zero even if there are no completions.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized context object.
[in] cpls Pointer to completion packet array.
[in] min Wait for at least this many completions.
[in] max The maximum number of completions to return.
Returns:
The number of commands completed and copied into the comps array or GXPCI_ERESET if the queue is reset by the remote end.

uint32_t gxpci_c2c_send_get_credits ( gxpci_context_t *  context  ) 

Retrieve the number of command credits, i.e.

new command descriptors that can be posted, of a chip-to-chip sender queue..

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized context object.
Returns:
The number of command credits in command queue on success.

uint32_t gxpci_c2c_recv_get_credits ( gxpci_context_t *  context  ) 

Retrieve the number of command credits, i.e.

new command descriptors that can be posted, of a chip-to-chip receiver queue..

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized context object.
Returns:
The number of command credits in command queue on success.

int gxpci_c2c_send_destroy ( gxpci_context_t *  context  ) 

Release the communication endpoint and free the allocated resources for a chip-to-chip sender queue.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized context object.
Returns:
0 on success, non-zero on error.

int gxpci_c2c_recv_destroy ( gxpci_context_t *  context  ) 

Release the communication endpoint and free the allocated resources for a chip-to-chip receiver queue.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized context object.
Returns:
0 on success, non-zero on error.

int gxpci_nic_init ( gxio_trio_context_t *  trio_context,
gxpci_nic_state_t *  nic_state,
unsigned int  trio_index,
unsigned int  mac,
int  nic_index,
int  asid 
)

The following prototypes are for the host NIC API implementation.

Open a host NIC interface.

Parameters:
[in] trio_context Pointer to a pre-allocated gxio_trio_context_t struct.
[in] nic_state Pointer to a pre-allocated gxpci_nic_state_t struct.
[in] trio_index The TRIO index of the local PCIe port, 0 or 1.
[in] mac The mac number of the local PCIe port, 0, 1 or 2.
[in] nic_index The index of this host NIC interface.
[in] asid The ASID if pre-allocated, else GXIO_ASID_NULL.
Returns:
0 on success, non-zero on error.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_host_nic/gxpci_echo_drv_module.c.

int gxpci_nic_t2h_queue_init ( gxio_trio_context_t *  trio_context,
gxpci_nic_state_t *  nic_state,
gxpci_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  queue_index 
)

Initialize a host NIC T2H queue.

Parameters:
[in] trio_context Pointer to a pre-allocated gxio_trio_context_t struct.
[in] nic_state Pointer to a pre-allocated gxpci_nic_state_t struct.
[in] context Initialized gxpci_context_t struct for the queue.
[in] queue_index The index of the T2H queue within this NIC.
Returns:
0 on success, non-zero on error.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_host_nic/gxpci_echo_drv_module.c.

int gxpci_nic_t2h_queue_complete_init ( gxpci_context_t *  context  ) 

Complete a host NIC T2H queue initialization.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized gxpci_context_t struct for the queue.
Returns:
0 on success.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_host_nic/gxpci_echo_drv_module.c.

int gxpci_nic_t2h_queue_complete_init_nb ( gxpci_context_t *  context  ) 

Check if a host NIC T2H queue initialization can be completed.

If not, this function needs to be called again.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized gxpci_context_t struct for the queue.
Returns:
0 on success, -EAGAIN if incompletion, GXPCI_ERESET if queue is reset.

int gxpci_nic_h2t_queue_init ( gxio_trio_context_t *  trio_context,
gxpci_nic_state_t *  nic_state,
gxpci_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  queue_index 
)

Initialize a host NIC H2T queue.

Parameters:
[in] trio_context Pointer to a pre-allocated gxio_trio_context_t struct.
[in] nic_state Pointer to a pre-allocated gxpci_nic_state_t struct.
[in] context Initialized gxpci_context_t struct for the queue.
[in] queue_index The index of the H2T queue within this NIC.
Returns:
0 on success, non-zero on error.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_host_nic/gxpci_echo_drv_module.c.

int gxpci_nic_h2t_queue_complete_init ( gxpci_context_t *  context  ) 

Complete a host NIC H2T queue initialization.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized gxpci_context_t struct for the queue.
Returns:
0 on success.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_host_nic/gxpci_echo_drv_module.c.

int gxpci_nic_h2t_queue_complete_init_nb ( gxpci_context_t *  context  ) 

Check if a host NIC H2T queue initialization can be completed.

If not, this function needs to be called again.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized gxpci_context_t struct for the queue.
Returns:
0 on success, -EAGAIN if incompletion, GXPCI_ERESET if queue is reset.

int gxpci_nic_init_vf ( gxio_trio_context_t *  trio_context,
gxpci_nic_state_t *  nic_state,
unsigned int  trio_index,
unsigned int  mac,
int  vf_index,
int  asid,
void *  backing_mem 
)

Open a host NIC interface on a Virtual Function.

Parameters:
[in] trio_context Pointer to a pre-allocated gxio_trio_context_t struct.
[in] nic_state Pointer to a pre-allocated gxpci_nic_state_t struct.
[in] trio_index The TRIO index of the local PCIe port, 0 or 1.
[in] mac The mac number of the local PCIe port, 0, 1 or 2.
[in] vf_index The index of associated VF.
[in] asid The ASID if pre-allocated, else GXIO_ASID_NULL.
[in] backing_mem The registered buffer that is used to store the VF NIC's registers and DMA descriptors.
Returns:
0 on success, non-zero on error.

int gxpci_nic_complete_init_vf ( gxpci_nic_state_t *  nic_state  ) 

Complete a host NIC initialization on a Virtual Function.

Parameters:
[in] nic_state Pointer to a pre-allocated gxpci_nic_state_t struct.
Returns:
0 on success.

int gxpci_nic_complete_init_vf_nb ( gxpci_nic_state_t *  nic_state  ) 

Check if a host NIC initialization can be completed on a Virtual Function.

If not, this function needs to be called again.

Parameters:
[in] nic_state Pointer to a pre-allocated gxpci_nic_state_t struct.
Returns:
0 on success, -EAGAIN if incompletion.

int gxpci_nic_t2h_queue_init_vf ( gxio_trio_context_t *  trio_context,
gxpci_nic_state_t *  nic_state,
gxpci_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  queue_index 
)

Initialize a host NIC T2H queue on a VF.

Parameters:
[in] trio_context Pointer to a pre-allocated gxio_trio_context_t struct.
[in] nic_state Pointer to a pre-allocated gxpci_nic_state_t struct.
[in] context Initialized gxpci_context_t struct for the queue.
[in] queue_index The index of the T2H queue within this NIC.
Returns:
0 on success, non-zero on error.

int gxpci_nic_t2h_queue_complete_init_vf ( gxpci_context_t *  context,
gxpci_nic_state_t *  nic_state 
)

Complete a host NIC T2H queue initialization on a VF.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized gxpci_context_t struct for the queue.
[in] nic_state Pointer to a pre-allocated gxpci_nic_state_t struct.
Returns:
0 on success.

int gxpci_nic_t2h_queue_complete_init_vf_nb ( gxpci_context_t *  context,
gxpci_nic_state_t *  nic_state 
)

Check if a host NIC T2H queue initialization on a VF can be completed.

If not, this function needs to be called again.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized gxpci_context_t struct for the queue.
[in] nic_state Pointer to a pre-allocated gxpci_nic_state_t struct.
Returns:
0 on success, -EAGAIN if incompletion, GXPCI_ERESET if queue is reset.

int gxpci_nic_h2t_queue_init_vf ( gxio_trio_context_t *  trio_context,
gxpci_nic_state_t *  nic_state,
gxpci_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  queue_index 
)

Initialize a host NIC H2T queue on a VF.

Parameters:
[in] trio_context Pointer to a pre-allocated gxio_trio_context_t struct.
[in] nic_state Pointer to a pre-allocated gxpci_nic_state_t struct.
[in] context Initialized gxpci_context_t struct for the queue.
[in] queue_index The index of the H2T queue within this NIC.
Returns:
0 on success, non-zero on error.

int gxpci_nic_h2t_queue_complete_init_vf ( gxpci_context_t *  context,
gxpci_nic_state_t *  nic_state 
)

Complete a host NIC H2T queue initialization on a VF.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized gxpci_context_t struct for the queue.
[in] nic_state Pointer to a pre-allocated gxpci_nic_state_t struct.
Returns:
0 on success.

int gxpci_nic_h2t_queue_complete_init_vf_nb ( gxpci_context_t *  context,
gxpci_nic_state_t *  nic_state 
)

Check if a host NIC H2T queue initialization on a VF can be completed.

If not, this function needs to be called again.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized gxpci_context_t struct for the queue.
[in] nic_state Pointer to a pre-allocated gxpci_nic_state_t struct.
Returns:
0 on success, -EAGAIN if incompletion, GXPCI_ERESET if queue is reset.

int gxpci_nic_t2h_cmds ( gxpci_context_t *  context,
const gxpci_cmd_t *  cmd,
unsigned int  count 
)

Post multiple commands to a host NIC T2H queue.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized gxpci_context_t struct.
[in] cmd Pointer to an array of commands.
[in] count Number of commands to enqueue.
Returns:
0 on success, or GXPCI_ERESET if the queue is reset.

int gxpci_nic_t2h_cmd ( gxpci_context_t *  context,
const gxpci_cmd_t *  cmd 
) [inline]

Post a single command to a host NIC T2H queue.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized gxpci_context_t struct.
[in] cmd Pointer to a command.
Returns:
0 on success, or GXPCI_ERESET if the queue is reset.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_host_nic/gxpci_echo_drv_module.c.

int gxpci_nic_h2t_cmds ( gxpci_context_t *  context,
const gxpci_cmd_t *  cmd,
unsigned int  count 
)

Post multiple commands to a host NIC H2T queue.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized gxpci_context_t struct.
[in] cmd Pointer to an array of commands.
[in] count Number of commands to enqueue.
Returns:
0 on success, or GXPCI_ERESET if the queue is reset.

int gxpci_nic_h2t_cmd ( gxpci_context_t *  context,
const gxpci_cmd_t *  cmd 
) [inline]

Post a single command to a host NIC T2H queue.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized gxpci_context_t struct.
[in] cmd Pointer to a command.
Returns:
0 on success, or GXPCI_ERESET if the queue is reset.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_host_nic/gxpci_echo_drv_module.c.

int gxpci_nic_get_comps ( gxpci_context_t *  context,
gxpci_nic_comp_t *  cpls,
int  min,
int  max 
)

Retrieve command completions from a host NIC T2H or H2T queue.

This function will block if there are fewer than the minimal number of completions which is specified by "min". It returns immediately if min is zero even if there are no completions.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized context object.
[in] cpls Pointer to completion packet array.
[in] min Wait for at least this many completions.
[in] max The maximum number of completions to return.
Returns:
The number of commands completed and copied into the comps array or GXPCI_ERESET if the queue is reset by the remote end.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_host_nic/gxpci_echo_drv_module.c.

int gxpci_nic_get_cmd_credits ( gxpci_context_t *  context  ) 

Retrieve the number of command credits, i.e.

new command descriptors that can be posted, of a host NIC H2T or T2H queue.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized context object.
Returns:
The number of command credits in command queue on success.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_host_nic/gxpci_echo_drv_module.c.

int gxpci_nic_destroy ( gxio_trio_context_t *  trio_context,
gxpci_nic_state_t *  nic_state 
)

Release the communication endpoint and free the allocated resources for a host NIC interface.

Parameters:
[in] trio_context Pointer to a pre-allocated gxio_trio_context_t struct.
[in] nic_state Pointer to a pre-allocated gxpci_nic_state_t struct.
Returns:
0 on success.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_host_nic/gxpci_echo_drv_module.c.

int gxpci_nic_destroy_vf ( gxio_trio_context_t *  trio_context,
gxpci_nic_state_t *  nic_state 
)

Release the communication endpoint and free the allocated resources for a host NIC VF interface.

Parameters:
[in] trio_context Pointer to a pre-allocated gxio_trio_context_t struct.
[in] nic_state Pointer to a pre-allocated gxpci_nic_state_t struct.
Returns:
0 on success.

int gxpci_raw_dma_init ( gxio_trio_context_t *  trio_context,
gxpci_raw_dma_state_t *  rd_state,
unsigned int  trio_index,
unsigned int  mac,
int  asid 
)

The following prototypes are for the host Raw DMA API implementation.

Open a host Raw DMA channel containing one or more T2H/H2T queues.

Parameters:
[in] trio_context Pointer to a pre-allocated gxio_trio_context_t struct.
[in] rd_state Pointer to a pre-allocated gxpci_raw_dma_state_t struct.
[in] trio_index The TRIO index of the local PCIe port, 0 or 1.
[in] mac The mac number of the local PCIe port, 0, 1 or 2.
[in] asid The ASID if pre-allocated, else GXIO_ASID_NULL.
Returns:
0 on success, non-zero on error.

int gxpci_open_raw_dma_recv_queue ( gxio_trio_context_t *  trio_context,
gxpci_raw_dma_state_t *  rd_state,
gxpci_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  queue_index 
)

Open a host Raw DMA receive (H2T) queue.

Parameters:
[in] trio_context Pointer to a pre-allocated gxio_trio_context_t struct.
[in] rd_state Pointer to a pre-allocated gxpci_raw_dma_state_t struct.
[in] context Initialized gxpci_context_t struct for the queue.
[in] queue_index The index of the H2T queue within the channel.
Returns:
0 on success, non-zero on error.

int gxpci_open_raw_dma_send_queue ( gxio_trio_context_t *  trio_context,
gxpci_raw_dma_state_t *  rd_state,
gxpci_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  queue_index 
)

Open a host Raw DMA send (T2H) queue.

Parameters:
[in] trio_context Pointer to a pre-allocated gxio_trio_context_t struct.
[in] rd_state Pointer to a pre-allocated gxpci_raw_dma_state_t struct.
[in] context Initialized gxpci_context_t struct for the queue.
[in] queue_index The index of the T2H queue within the channel.
Returns:
0 on success, non-zero on error.

unsigned int gxpci_raw_dma_get_host_buf_size ( gxpci_context_t *  context  ) 

Obtain the host Raw DMA buffer size.

Tile side application can use this value to determine the upper bound of the target buffer. This function should be called after the queue is opened.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized gxpci_context_t struct.
Returns:
host buffer size in bytes.

unsigned int gxpci_raw_dma_get_host_counter ( gxpci_context_t *  context  ) 

Obtain the host Raw DMA counter that is updated by the host side application.

Tile side application can use this value for flow-control purpose. The exact FC algorithm is defined by the application.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized gxpci_context_t struct.
Returns:
a unsigned integer value.

int gxpci_raw_dma_recv_get_comps ( gxpci_context_t *  context,
gxpci_comp_t *  cpls,
int  min,
int  max 
)

Retrieve command completions from a Raw DMA receive queue.

This function will block if there are fewer than the minimal number of completions which is specified by "min". It returns immediately if min is zero even if there are no completions.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized context object.
[in] cpls Pointer to completion array.
[in] min Wait for at least this many completions.
[in] max The maximum number of completions to return.
Returns:
The number of commands completed and copied into the comps array or GXPCI_ERESET if the queue is reset by the remote end.

int gxpci_raw_dma_send_get_comps ( gxpci_context_t *  context,
gxpci_comp_t *  cpls,
int  min,
int  max 
)

Retrieve command completions from a Raw DMA send queue.

This function will block if there are fewer than the minimal number of completions which is specified by "min". It returns immediately if min is zero even if there are no completions.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized context object.
[in] cpls Pointer to completion array.
[in] min Wait for at least this many completions.
[in] max The maximum number of completions to return.
Returns:
The number of commands completed and copied into the comps array or GXPCI_ERESET if the queue is reset by the remote end.

unsigned int gxpci_raw_dma_recv_get_credits ( gxpci_context_t *  context  ) 

Retrieve the number of command credits, i.e.

new command descriptors that can be posted, of a Raw DMA receive queue.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized context object.
Returns:
The number of command credits in command queue on success.

unsigned int gxpci_raw_dma_send_get_credits ( gxpci_context_t *  context  ) 

Retrieve the number of command credits, i.e.

new command descriptors that can be posted, of a Raw DMA send queue.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized context object.
Returns:
The number of command credits in command queue on success.

int gxpci_raw_dma_recv_destroy ( gxpci_context_t *  context  ) 

Close a Raw DMA receive queue.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized context object.
Returns:
0 on success.

int gxpci_raw_dma_send_destroy ( gxpci_context_t *  context  ) 

Close a Raw DMA send queue.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized context object.
Returns:
0 on success.

int gxpci_raw_dma_destroy ( gxio_trio_context_t *  trio_context,
gxpci_raw_dma_state_t *  rd_state 
)

Release the communication endpoint and free the allocated resources for a host Raw DMA channel.

This function should be called after each queue has returned from gxpci_raw_dma_send_destroy() or gxpci_raw_dma_recv_destroy().

Parameters:
[in] trio_context Pointer to a pre-allocated gxio_trio_context_t struct.
[in] rd_state Pointer to a pre-allocated gxpci_raw_dma_state_t struct.
Returns:
0 on success.

int gxpci_open_pq_h2t_queue_vf ( gxpci_context_t *  context,
int  asid,
struct gxpci_packet_queue_state *  queue_state,
unsigned int  vf_instance,
unsigned int  queue_index,
void *  backing_mem 
)

Open a Packet Queue H2T queue between an SR-IOV Virtual Function and a remote PCIe port on the host.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized context object.
[in] asid The pre-allocated ASID.
[in] queue_state The pre-allocated gxpci_packet_queue_state object.
[in] vf_instance The VF instance number.
[in] queue_index The index of the queue.
[in] backing_mem The registered buffer that is used to store the Pull DMA descriptor rings.
Returns:
0 on success, -EBUSY if the queue resource is not available, GXPCI_ERESET if the queue is reset by the remote end, or an error value of type gxio_err_e.

int gxpci_open_pq_t2h_queue_vf ( gxpci_context_t *  context,
int  asid,
struct gxpci_packet_queue_state *  queue_state,
unsigned int  vf_instance,
unsigned int  queue_index,
void *  backing_mem 
)

Open a Packet Queue T2H queue between an SR-IOV Virtual Function and a remote PCIe port on the host.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized context object.
[in] asid The pre-allocated ASID.
[in] queue_state The pre-allocated gxpci_packet_queue_state object.
[in] vf_instance The VF instance number.
[in] queue_index The index of the queue.
[in] backing_mem The registered buffer that is used to store the Push DMA descriptor rings.
Returns:
0 on success, -EBUSY if the queue resource is not available, GXPCI_ERESET if the queue is reset by the remote end, or an error value of type gxio_err_e.

int gxpci_open_pq_duplex_queue_vf ( gxpci_context_t *  h2t_context,
gxpci_context_t *  t2h_context,
int  asid,
struct gxpci_packet_queue_state *  h2t_queue_state,
struct gxpci_packet_queue_state *  t2h_queue_state,
unsigned int  vf_instance,
unsigned int  queue_index,
void *  h2t_backing_mem,
void *  t2h_backing_mem 
)

Open a selected duplex queue between an SR-IOV Virtual Function and a remote PCIe port.

Parameters:
[in] h2t_context Initialized context object of H2T direction for queue type GXPCI_PQ_DUPLEX_VF.
[in] t2h_context Initialized context object of T2H direction for queue type GXPCI_PQ_DUPLEX_VF.
[in] asid The ASID if pre-allocated, else GXIO_ASID_NULL.
[in] h2t_queue_state The pre-allocated gxpci_packet_queue_state object for the H2T queue.
[in] t2h_queue_state The pre-allocated gxpci_packet_queue_state object for the T2H queue.
[in] vf_instance The VF instance number.
[in] queue_index The index of the duplex queue for queue type GXPCI_PQ_DUPLEX_VF.
[in] h2t_backing_mem The registered buffer that is used to store the Pull DMA descriptor rings for the H2T queue.
[in] t2h_backing_mem The registered buffer that is used to store the Push DMA descriptor rings for the T2H queue.
Returns:
0 on success, -EBUSY if the queue resource is not available, GXPCI_ERESET if the queue is reset by the remote end, or an error value of type gxio_err_e.

int gxpci_pq_destroy ( gxpci_context_t *  context  ) 

Release the communication endpoint and free the allocated resources for the Packet Queue interfaces.

Parameters:
[in] context Initialized context object.
Returns:
0 on success, non-zero on error.
